# Maryam Nawaz did not say her daughter graduated at the age of 13

## A clip of her speech at a recent conference went viral with a false caption

Claim: CM Punjab Maryam Nawaz claimed during a speech that her daughter graduated at the age of 13.

Fact: Nawaz did not say this. The caption of the video is false.

geo_factcheck's profile picture

geo\_factcheck

A video of Punjab Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz Sharif is being widely shared on social media with the claim that she said her youngest daughter graduated from college at the age of 13 during a recent speech.  
   
The claim is false. Sharif did not say her daughter graduated at the age of 13. In her speech, she said her daughter has recently graduated and recalled that she was 13-years-old when she visited her mother in prison.

sources.title: Maryam Nawaz Age, Daughter, Son, Husband & Education
sources.content:
### Maryam Nawaz Age

Maryam Nawaz age is 52 Years old. She was born on October 28, 1973, in Lahore, Pakistan. Maryam Nawaz age was 19 years old when she got married and 39 years old when she entered politics in 2012. She was first tasked with managing the 2013 Pakistani general elections. Maryam became the chief minister of Punjab in 2024, at the age of 50.

### Maryam Nawaz Daughter

Maryam Nawaz Daughters are Mahnoor Safdar and Mehrunnisa Safdar. There is no more information available about M

reasoning: Multiple trusted sources confirm that Maryam Nawaz did not say her daughter graduated at the age of 13, and that the claim is false due to a misleading caption on a viral video.

sources.title: Senior citizens exemption
sources.content:
# Senior citizens exemption

Local governments and school districts in New York State can opt to grant a reduction on the amount of property taxes paid by qualifying senior citizens. This is accomplished by reducing the taxable assessment of the senior's home by as much as 50%.

To qualify, seniors generally must be 65 years of age or older and meet certain income limitations and other requirements. For the 50% exemption, the law allows each county, city, town, village, or school district to set

sources.title: Tax Relief for New York Seniors
sources.content:
1. Senior Citizens’ Exemption (Real Property Tax Law, section 467) The Senior Citizens’ Exemption reduces the property taxes of qualifying seniors by 50%. To qualify, homeowners must be at least 65 years old and meet certain income requirements. This exemption helps seniors afford to stay in their homes by lowering their annual property tax bills.
2. Enhanced STAR (School Tax Relief) Exemption The Enhanced STAR Exemption is available to seniors aged 65 and older whose income does not exceed the

sources.title: States That Exempt Senior Citizens From Property Taxes
sources.content:
Mississippi: Exempts $7,500 of assessed home value for homeowners aged 65-plus.
 Nebraska: Offers property tax reductions for seniors on their primary residence.
 New York: Local governments may exempt up to 50% of assessed value for seniors with incomes under roughly $58,000. Seniors may also qualify for Enhanced STAR school tax relief.
 North Carolina: Exempts the greater of $25,000 or 50% of the home’s assessed value.
 Ohio: Exempts $26,200 of assessed value for qualified senior homeowners.

sources.title: 17 states that offer property tax relief for seniors | Rocket Mortgage
sources.content:
### New York

New York allows local governments to exempt up to 50% of a qualifying senior’s home assessed value from property taxes for homeowners aged 65 and older. Income limits are set locally and can vary widely, typically ranging up to $50,000 for the full exemption, with some jurisdictions offering partial exemptions at higher income levels. Applicants must generally own and occupy the property as their primary residence and meet local income requirements. Separate programs, such as Enhan

Note:

Section 194P of the Income Tax Act, 1961 provides conditions for exempting Senior Citizens from filing income tax returns aged 75 years and above.

Conditions for exemption are:

As per income tax law, an individual is treated as senior citizen on attainment of age of 60 years and above and a super senior citizen on attainment of age of 80 years and above at any time up to the last date of the respective financial year. Some sources mention exemptions for seniors, but the age requirements and conditions vary, and none of them explicitly state a 50% exemption for citizens above 60 years of age.

sources.content: What casual users likely don’t know is that the Pakistan Telecommunication Authority (PTA) has announced a registration requirement for all Virtual Private Networks (VPNs) by 30th June 2020. This is twenty-two days after they first posted a public service announcement on their website. The PTA regulations do not ban the use of VPNs entirely, but they do require users to register their VPN use with their Internet Service Providers (ISPs). To do this they must share their CNIC number, the purpose

sources.title: Is VPN Legal in Pakistan? (Clear Answer for 2026)
sources.content:
## What Does PTA Actually Say About VPNs?

The Pakistan Telecommunication Authority (PTA) allows VPN usage but requires registration of VPNs used for professional or business purposes.

This policy exists mainly to:

 Prevent misuse
 Monitor corporate traffic
 Allow businesses to operate smoothly
 Protect national network infrastructure

PTA has repeatedly stated that VPN registration is for organizations, not casual users.

## Who Is Required to Register a VPN with PTA?

sources.content:
To combat confusion among voters and to ease access to election-related information, the party launched an online portal on its website insaf.pk, as well as a “back-up site”, pticandidates.com.

It also introduced a feature whereby voters can message on Imran Khan’s official Facebook account to get information about PTI-backed candidates.

Dawn.com staffers tried accessing the PTI’s websites on Friday but were unable to do so through Wi-Fi or mobile data, except when using a Virtual Private Netw

sources.title: Internet censorship in Pakistan - Wikipedia
sources.content:
Pakistan has blocked access to websites critical of the government or the military. Blocking of websites is often carried out under the rubric of restricting access to "blasphemous" content, pornography, or religious immorality. At the end of 2011, the PTA had officially banned more than 1,000 pornographic websites in Pakistan.

## Political Censorship

The second party to be banned by the PTI was the far-right religious outfit Tehreek-e-Labbaik Pakistan, which was banned in May 2021 following protests in Punjab province that turned violent, killing several policemen. sources.title: Libel, Slander, and Defamation Law: The Basics - FindLaw
sources.content:
## Social Media and Defamation Law

Social media makes it easier than ever to make a defamatory statement. Social media services like Twitter and Facebook allow you to instantly “publish” a statement that can reach millions of people. Whether it’s a disparaging blog post, Facebook status update, or YouTube video, online defamation is treated the same way as more traditional forms. You can be sued for any defamatory statements you post online.

sources.title: Two-party system | Political Science | Research Starters
sources.content:
RESEARCH STARTER

# Two-party system

A two-party system is a political structure characterized by the dominance of two major political parties that compete for power, typically alternating control of government through elections. The origins of this system can be traced back to eighteenth-century Europe and it has been adopted in various forms across different countries, notably in the United States and the United Kingdom. In such systems, voters primarily choose between these two parties, whic
